skill.name: using-neon
skill.description: Guides and best practices for working with Neon Serverless Postgres. Covers getting started, local development with Neon, choosing a connection method, Neon features, authentication (@neondatabase/auth), PostgREST-style data API (@neondatabase/neon-js), Neon CLI, and Neon's Platform API/SDKs. Use for any Neon-related questions.
skill.summary: Guides and best practices for working with Neon Serverless Postgres. Covers getting started, local d...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many backtick patterns, hardcoded URLs, and weak cryptography indicators. Review found these are Markdown examples, official Neon documentation URLs, and prose false positives, with no evidence found of prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Official Documentation Fetch Command
low_findings.description: The skill includes curl examples that fetch Neon documentation from neon.com. This is legitimate documentation lookup, but it still directs network access to an external service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The curl commands are explicit and point to official Neon documentation. No credential collection, user data upload, or suspicious destination is present.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Flags Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Most external command findings are inline Markdown references to files, packages, or CLI topics. They are not executable shell or Ruby backtick expressions in code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content appears inside Markdown tables and prose. There is no script file, interpreter context, or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Flags Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static blocker findings do not correspond to cryptographic code. The referenced lines contain skill metadata or headings, not MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are visible prose and metadata. No cryptographic function, digest call, or security-sensitive hashing operation appears there.

content.value_statement: Neon setup and integration choices can be hard to verify across tools and runtimes. This skill guides assistants to use Neon documentation and choose suitable Postgres workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Neon Serverless Postgres.
Points assistants to official Neon documentation before making claims.
Lists guidance areas for setup, branching, autoscaling, and restore features.
Covers connection method selection for drivers, ORMs, and serverless runtimes.
Identifies Neon Auth, Neon JS SDK, Platform API, CLI, and SDK topics.
limitations: The skill depends on external Neon documentation for current details.
It does not include the referenced resource files in the scanned file structure.
It does not execute database migrations or create Neon projects by itself.
It cannot verify account permissions or production configuration without user-provided context.

use_cases.title: Plan a Neon Project Setup
use_cases.description: Use the skill to choose a Neon setup path, connection string approach, and first database tasks.
use_cases.target_user: Application developers starting a new Postgres project

use_cases.title: Compare Runtime Connection Options
use_cases.description: Use the skill to decide between serverless, WebSocket, ORM, and standard Postgres connection patterns.
use_cases.target_user: Backend engineers deploying across edge and server environments

use_cases.title: Review Neon Automation Choices
use_cases.description: Use the skill to identify relevant CLI, Platform API, and SDK documentation for automated workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Start with Neon
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a new application with Neon Serverless Postgres. Explain the first decisions I should make.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Connection Method
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare Neon connection methods for my runtime and ORM. Recommend a practical option and explain the tradeoffs.
prompt_templates.scenario: Selecting database access for an application

prompt_templates.title: Use Neon Features
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan how to use Neon branching, autoscaling, scale-to-zero, and instant restore for my development workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Design Neon Automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Map the Neon CLI, Platform API, and SDK options for automating project, branch, and database management.

output_examples.input: Which Neon connection approach should I use for an edge function?
output_examples.output: A concise comparison of serverless-friendly options, driver constraints, and documentation pages to verify before implementation.

output_examples.input: How should my team use Neon branching during development?
output_examples.output: A workflow outline covering branch creation, preview environments, review steps, and cleanup considerations.

output_examples.input: What Neon tools should I use for CI automation?
output_examples.output: A recommendation that separates CLI tasks, Platform API tasks, SDK choices, and security checks for credentials.

best_practices: Verify Neon-specific claims against official documentation before implementation.
Choose connection methods based on runtime, driver support, and pooling needs.
Keep Neon credentials out of prompts, logs, and shared examples.
anti_patterns: Do not guess documentation URLs when the Neon index should be checked first.
Do not treat every Postgres pattern as suitable for serverless runtimes.
Do not expose Neon connection strings while asking for setup help.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps assistants answer Neon Serverless Postgres questions using documented setup, connection, feature, API, and CLI guidance.

faq.question: Does it create Neon databases automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and documentation direction, but it does not create resources by itself.

faq.question: Why does the audit mention network access?
faq.answer: The skill includes curl examples for fetching official Neon documentation from neon.com.

faq.question: Can it help choose a database driver?
faq.answer: Yes. It points assistants toward connection method, serverless driver, Drizzle ORM, and SDK guidance.

faq.question: Can it answer current Neon product questions?
faq.answer: It instructs assistants to verify Neon claims against official documentation before answering.

faq.question: What security care should users take?
faq.answer: Users should avoid sharing Neon connection strings, API keys, and production credentials in prompts.
